id: "19.2.15"
title: "HCI Proactive Rebalance Job Progress and Per-Disk Skew Tracking"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.15 · HCI Proactive Rebalance Job Progress and Per-Disk Skew Tracking

## Description

Monitors HCI scheduled and proactive rebalance job progress while clusters remain healthy: Nutanix Curator scans and jobs, vSAN proactive rebalance posture (not component resync), HyperFlex rebalance progress, and Azure Stack HCI Optimize-StoragePool or Optimize-Volume jobs with computed percent slope ETA, disk utilization skew, stall detection when percent stops advancing, and governance against disabled proactive policies with lingering skew. This deliberately contrasts UC-19.2.15 with UC-19.2.30, which tracks failure-driven vSAN component resync backlog depth and adaptive pacing; with UC-19.2.9, which covers post-failure rebuild and data resiliency restoration timelines; and with UC-19.2.8, which highlights steady-state per-node load skew without binding results to an active vendor rebalance job identifier or scan lifecycle.

## Value

Planned and proactive rebalances reclaim capacity before failures force urgent evacuations. When job percent stalls while disks stay uneven, or when proactive policy is off while utilization spread exceeds policy, reclaim work stops and the next failure or growth spike lands in a thinner margin. Tracking ETA from recent slope, highlighting skew during the same window, and counting proactive completions over seven days gives operations and capacity teams an early, vendor-neutral narrative for scheduling maintenance, approving poller changes, and proving hygiene work actually finished.

## Implementation

Install Splunk_TA_nutanix and custom Prism curator pollers, deploy PowerCLI exports for vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance and vmware:vsan:cluster_config, ingest HX Connect hyperflex:cluster:rebalance, schedule Azure Stack HCI Get-StorageJob and Get-PhysicalDisk exports, publish hci_rebalance_owner.csv with transforms.conf, paste the multisearch SPL into a fifteen-to-thirty-minute saved search, validate vendor consoles versus Splunk fields, and route severities with freeze-aware paging.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

This use case tracks scheduled and proactive storage rebalance jobs on healthy hyperconverged clusters during normal operations: Nutanix Curator full, partial, and selective scans plus optional per-disk balance telemetry; VMware vSAN proactive rebalance runtime flags and cluster threshold posture from PowerCLI exports; Cisco HyperFlex cluster rebalance state and progress from HX Connect REST; Azure Stack HCI Storage Spaces Direct Optimize-StoragePool and Optimize-Volume jobs with physical disk utilization snapshots. It is deliberately not UC-19.2.30, which measures failure-driven vSAN component resync queue depth and adaptive repair pacing. It is not UC-19.2.9, which covers resiliency loss and rebuild progress after node or disk failure. It is not UC-19.2.8, which compares steady-state per-node workload skew without tying the signal to an active Curator scan, vSAN proactive rebalance job, HyperFlex rebalance window, or S2D optimize job identifier. UC-19.2.15 isolates the operational story of capacity rebalancing that vendors trigger as hygiene: job percent complete, inferred time to finish from recent slope, stall detection when percent stops moving, proactive policy disabled while skew remains beyond governance, and a rolling seven-day completion counter for proactive-style jobs.

Indexes: standardize on index hci for merged payloads, or OR split indexes such as nutanix, vmware, cisco, and microsoft as role-based access control requires. HTTP Event Collector tokens should be scoped, rotated, and separated from interactive analyst tokens. On indexers and heavy forwarders, raise TRUNCATE for large JSON curator payloads, set SHOULD_LINEMERGE false, and prefer INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS json when your forwarder emits raw JSON with stable keys. Time must land in UTC on poll_utc or equivalent so job age math and streamstats windows align across regions.

Publish lookups/hci_rebalance_owner.csv with transforms.conf stanza hci_rebalance_owner, min_matches zero, case_sensitive_match aligned with your cluster_id naming standard. Required columns: cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_max_eta_hours, target_max_disk_skew_pct, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct, target_max_stall_hours, change_freeze_until. Defaults in SPL mirror twelve hours max ETA, fifteen percent disk skew, thirty percent proactive threshold floor, and four stall hours when cells are empty. Vendors in this file use short slugs nutanix, vsan, hyperflex, and azure_stack_hci to join cleanly; normalize strings in the lookup to match exporter output.

Splunk_TA_nutanix (Splunkbase 5076) provides nutanix:curator; extend with pollers for Prism v2 curator scans and jobs posting nutanix:curator:scan, nutanix:curator:job, and nutanix:curator:disk_balance. Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (5380) complements PowerCLI exports into vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance and vmware:vsan:cluster_config. Hyperflex:cluster:rebalance arrives from HX Connect. Azure Stack HCI exports land as azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job, azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance, and azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk. Document field names your scripts emit so coalesce lists stay short and auditable.

Service accounts must be read-only for Prism, vCenter, HX Connect, and WinRM or cluster PowerShell remoting. Secrets belong in vaults, not saved searches. Paging policy should treat stalled proactive jobs with high skew as higher urgency than a long but still advancing job during a change freeze window where severity is demoted to info while remaining visible.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Nutanix: install Splunk_TA_nutanix on collectors with Prism Element reachability on TCP 9440. Enable nutanix:curator inputs per documentation. Add a Python or PowerShell poller that authenticates to /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster/curator/scans and /v2.0/cluster/curator/jobs/{job_id}, emitting one JSON line per poll per object with sourcetype nutanix:curator:scan or nutanix:curator:job. Add a second poller for per-disk balance if you expose it separately as nutanix:curator:disk_balance with disk_used_pct populated per disk serial or disk id. Map cluster_id from cluster uuid or name consistently with the configuration management database. Map job_id from scan id or job id strings. Map rebalance_pct from vendor percent complete fields. Map job_started_ts from epoch seconds when available, else derive from first-seen time in a staging summary. Tag completion events for selective versus full scans so seven-day completion counts stay meaningful.

VMware vSAN proactive posture: schedule PowerShell on a management host using VMware.PowerCLI. Export Get-VsanRuntimeInfo fields ProactiveRebalanceRunning and ProactiveRebalanceMaxIOPS per cluster or host as your schema dictates, sourcetype vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance. Export Get-VsanClusterConfiguration proactive rebalance threshold defaulting around thirty percent as vmware:vsan:cluster_config with cluster_id and proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct. Do not reuse vmware:vsan:resync:component from UC-19.2.30; proactive rebalance is a distinct code path. Post JSON via HEC with TLS and acknowledgment when supported.

Cisco HyperFlex: authenticate to HX Connect over HTTPS. Call /rest/clusters/{cid}/rebalance/state and /rest/clusters/{cid}/rebalance/progress on your approved cadence, sourcetype hyperflex:cluster:rebalance. Flatten progress percent, job or operation ids, and timestamps into cluster_id and job_id columns that match your configuration management database keys.

Azure Stack HCI: schedule Export-SplunkJson style scripts on a jump host with cluster rights. Invoke Get-StorageJob for jobs named Optimize-StoragePool or Optimize-Volume, sourcetypes azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job and azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance as you split concerns. Run Get-PhysicalDisk with Select-Object Property star on an interval for azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk, emitting disk_used_pct as percent used or derived used versus size. Keep hostnames and serial fields only if policy allows.

props.conf should codify TIME_PREFIX on poll_utc when present. Add FIELDALIAS only after reviewing fieldsummary across an upgrade window so you do not double-map keys.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL below exactly as uc_19_2_15_hci_proactive_rebalance_signal unless your catalog mandates another name. Schedule every fifteen to thirty minutes with time bounds earliest=-25h@h latest=@h so streamstats windows have history. Alert when severity is critical or high and freeze is inactive. Throttle per cluster_id and vendor for forty-five minutes when severity is stable warning. Route critical to platform storage on-call; route high to infrastructure queue with mandate to confirm vendor console matches Splunk slope math.

Governance: keep target_max_eta_hours, target_max_disk_skew_pct, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct, and target_max_stall_hours in the CSV so thresholds evolve without SPL edits. When poll cadence is faster than hourly, reduce interpretive risk by correlating stall minutes with wall clock in a follow-on summary search.

Canonical SPL for operator paste:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.15 HCI proactive scheduled rebalance progress and per-disk skew on healthy clusters; distinct from UC-19.2.30 resync backlog, UC-19.2.9 failure rebuild, UC-19.2.8 steady skew without job context. Tunables: hci_rebalance_owner.csv (target_max_eta_hours default 12, target_max_disk_skew_pct default 15, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct default 30, target_max_stall_hours default 4). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes to info when active. Each arm: earliest=-25h@h latest=@h. Align streamstats window=24 with poller cadence.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:scan" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:job") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, scan_id, scanId, curator_job_id, curatorJobId, task_id, taskId, "nutanix_curator"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, rebalancePercent, percent_complete, scan_percent_complete, progress_pct, pct_complete, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(job_started_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(started_ts),10), relative_time(now(), "-25h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster_config") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, proactive_job_id, "vsan_proactive"))
      | eval pr_run=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceRunning, proactive_rebalance_running, proactiveRunning, "0")), 10)
      | eval pr_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceMaxIOPS, proactive_rebalance_max_iops, max_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval cfg_thr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceThresholdPct, proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct, proactive_threshold_pct, vsan_proactive_threshold_pct, "")), 10)
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, proactive_pct_complete, pct_complete, if((pr_run>0), 50, 0))), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(job_started_epoch),10), relative_time(now(), "-2h"))
      | eval proactive_cfg_thr=if(isnotnull(cfg_thr) AND cfg_thr>0, cfg_thr, null())
      | eval proactive_mark=if(pr_run>0, 1, 0)
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts latest(pr_run) AS pr_run latest(pr_iops) AS pr_iops latest(proactive_cfg_thr) AS proactive_cfg_thr max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance" earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, rebalance_job_id, operation_id, "hyperflex_rebalance"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, rebalancePercent, progress_pct, percentComplete, completion_pct, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(started_ts),10), relative_time(now(), "-6h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, clusterId, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, job_guid, JobId, Name, storage_job_id, "s2d_optimize"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, PercentComplete, percent_complete, progress_pct, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), relative_time(now(), "-12h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
| where (len(cluster_id)>0) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| search (index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator:disk_balance" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterUuid, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
     | eval vendor=case(sourcetype="nutanix:curator:disk_balance", "nutanix", sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk", "azure_stack_hci", "unknown")
     | eval disk_used_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(disk_used_pct, used_pct, utilization_pct, percent_used, SizeUsedPercent, "0")), 10)
     | where disk_used_pct>0
     | stats range(disk_used_pct) AS rebalance_disk_skew_pct BY cluster_id vendor ]
| fillnull value=0 rebalance_disk_skew_pct
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| search (index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=vmware OR index=cisco OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:scan" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:job" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance" OR sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance") earliest=-7d@d latest=@h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterId, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
     | eval vendor=case(match(sourcetype, "nutanix"), "nutanix", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance", "vsan", sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance", "hyperflex", match(sourcetype, "azure_stack_hci"), "azure_stack_hci", "unknown")
     | eval completed=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(completion_flag, job_complete, is_complete, scan_completed, Completed, status_code, "0")), 10)
     | where (completed>0) OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(status, job_state, JobState, ""))), "success|complete|succeeded")
     | stats count AS rebalance_jobs_completed_7d BY cluster_id vendor ]
| fillnull value=0 rebalance_jobs_completed_7d
| eval job_started_ts=if(isnull(job_started_ts), relative_time(now(), "-24h"), job_started_ts)
| eval rebalance_job_age_hours=round((now()-job_started_ts)/3600.0, 2)
| eval proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(proactive_cfg_thr, "")), 10)
| eval proactive_disabled=if((vendor="vsan") AND (pr_run=0), 1, 0)
| eventstats avg(rebalance_pct) AS cluster_mean_rebalance_pct max(rebalance_disk_skew_pct) AS cluster_context_max_skew BY cluster_id
| sort 0 cluster_id job_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t first(rebalance_pct) AS p_first last(rebalance_pct) AS p_last first(_time) AS t_first last(_time) AS t_last count AS win_cnt BY cluster_id job_id vendor
| eval slope=if((t_last>t_first) AND ((t_last-t_first)>60), (p_last-p_first)/((t_last-t_first)/60.0), null())
| eval rebalance_eta_minutes=if((isnotnull(slope)) AND (slope>0.02) AND (p_last<100), round(((100-p_last)/slope), 2), null())
| streamstats window=24 current=t min(rebalance_pct) AS rmin max(rebalance_pct) AS rmax BY cluster_id job_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_rebalance_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_eta_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_eta_hours, "12")), 10)
     | eval target_max_disk_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_disk_skew_pct, "15")), 10)
     | eval target_min_proactive_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_proactive_threshold_pct, "30")), 10)
     | eval target_max_stall_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stall_hours, "4")), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_eta_hours target_max_disk_skew_pct target_min_proactive_threshold_pct target_max_stall_hours change_freeze_until ]
| eval tmaxm=target_max_eta_hours*60.0
| eval stall_need=if(target_max_stall_hours>24, 24, target_max_stall_hours)
| eval rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator=if((win_cnt>=stall_need) AND (isnotnull(rmin)) AND (rmax=rmin) AND (rmax>0) AND (rmax<95), 1, 0)
| eval proactive_thr=if(isnotnull(proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct) AND proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct>0, proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct)
| eval proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator=if((vendor="vsan") AND (proactive_disabled=1) AND (rebalance_disk_skew_pct>proactive_thr), 1, 0)
| eval sev_base=case(((rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator=1) AND (rebalance_disk_skew_pct>=target_max_disk_skew_pct)), "critical", ((isnotnull(rebalance_eta_minutes)) AND (rebalance_eta_minutes>tmaxm)) OR (proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator=1), "high", ((rebalance_disk_skew_pct>=(target_max_disk_skew_pct-5)) AND (rebalance_pct<95)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev_base)
| table vendor cluster_id job_id rebalance_pct rebalance_eta_minutes rebalance_disk_skew_pct rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator severity owner_team change_freeze_until
```

Step 3 — Validate

Bring up each vendor console side by side with Splunk for the same fifteen-minute wall clock. Nutanix Prism should show Curator scan or job identifiers that match job_id and percent fields that align with rebalance_pct after unit normalization. vCenter should show proactive rebalance running flags consistent with vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance lines, and cluster configuration thresholds that match proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct. HX Connect rebalance panes should match hyperflex:cluster:rebalance progress. Windows Admin Center or Get-StorageJob on the Azure Stack HCI cluster should match azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job percent complete.

Validate disk skew by hand: pick two disks with lowest and highest used percent in the vendor interface and subtract; the number should approximate rebalance_disk_skew_pct within rounding. If skew is zero because phys_disk polling is offline, expect lookup-driven severity to focus on job ETA and stall math instead.

Replay synthetic fixtures into a lab index: ramp rebalance_pct linearly across synthetic polls and confirm rebalance_eta_minutes decreases toward a plausible remainder; hold percent flat across more than target_max_stall_hours samples and confirm rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator becomes one while slope nears null.

Confirm hci_rebalance_owner rows exist before production scheduling; a missing row still runs but thresholds fall back to defaults, which may be loose for regulated estates.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard rows: trellis single value rebalance_pct by cluster_id, sparkline of rebalance_pct over twenty-four hours per job_id, paired bar for rebalance_disk_skew_pct with threshold lines, table for rebalance_eta_minutes and rebalance_job_age_hours. Add a supplemental tile rebalance_jobs_completed_7d for monthly hygiene reviews.

Runbook tie-in: when UC-19.2.8 fires first, open UC-19.2.15 to see whether a proactive job exists and is advancing; when UC-19.2.30 fires, do not reinterpret that resync backlog as this proactive signal; keep failure narrative under UC-19.2.9 and UC-19.2.30. For HyperFlex and Azure Stack HCI, pair with change tickets that add or retire capacity because proactive jobs often follow shelf additions.

Evidence retention: export alert results weekly to a restricted index with owner_team, cluster_id, job_id, severity snapshot for internal audit. Version control hci_rebalance_owner with tickets.

Training: walk new engineers through interpreting slope-based ETA versus vendor ETA fields so incidents do not argue about minutes when both are estimates.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Case 1 Missing disk skew on HyperFlex or vSAN rows**: HyperFlex disk percent may not be in disk_balance feeds; extend collectors to emit per-disk utilization or accept skew from azure_stack_hci and nutanix only until a supplemental use case lands. Do not infer skew from cluster averages alone.

• **Case 2 ETA minutes null while vendor shows hours**: Slope below epsilon when polls flatten or percent stalls; widen streamstats window modestly or tighten poll cadence; verify job_started_ts is not stuck at synthetic defaults.

• **Case 3 Stall indicator always zero**: stall_need may exceed win_cnt because history shorter than twenty-five hours; backfill index or lower schedule while preserving governance intent; confirm multiple samples exist per job_id.

• **Case 4 proactive_disabled_with_skew never triggers**: cluster_config lines must co-exist in index with pr_run zero; confirm sourcetype split and timestamp alignment; verify vendor slug vsan matches lookup vendor column.

• **Case 5 Severity stuck info during real issues**: change_freeze_until may be parsing incorrectly; validate ISO-8601 strings; compare now() timezone assumptions; trim hidden characters in CSV.

• **Case 6 Seven-day completion count explodes**: completion regex too broad and counts non-proactive jobs; tighten where clause to sourcetypes and job names you classified as proactive; dedupe job_id in upstream exporter.

• **Case 7 Join drops rows after multisearch**: cluster_id normalization mismatch between arms and lookup; enforce one canonical id across CSV and exporters; add interim translation lookup if legacy managed object ids persist.

• **Case 8 Nutanix job_id churn breaks streamstats**: exporter rotates identifiers each poll; stabilize job_id to vendor stable scan id, not ephemeral task wrappers.

Extended notes for platform engineers: when Splunk upgrades change case sensitivity in transforms, re-validate min_matches behavior. When Prism upgrades alter JSON nesting, capture before-and-after samples in Git. When vSAN patches rename cmdlet output, adjust PowerCLI field maps before decommissioning old keys. When Storage Spaces Direct jobs linger at ninety-nine percent trim phases, tune warning predicates with business approval so warnings reflect customer risk, not cosmetic percent display.

Closing checklist: multisearch four arms present; coalesce chains reviewed; join-based hci_rebalance_owner ingest present; no raw lookup command; disk skew join uses disk-only sourcetypes; outer pipeline uses eventstats for cluster context; streamstats present for ETA and stall; outer ends with table projection; freeze demotion verified quarterly in tabletop exercises.

Supplement A — Capacity planning exports: archive monthly CSV of rebalance_eta_minutes peaks even when severity stays warning. Supplement B — Security: restrict who edits hci_rebalance_owner because thresholds gate pages. Supplement C — Performance: acceleration rarely helps multisearch unions; reduce poll frequency before enabling summary indexing. Supplement D — Naming: keep equipmentModels aligned with uc.schema patterns. Supplement E — Documentation: link this JSON from Confluence with data owner roster. Supplement F — Multisite: duplicate rows per site if cluster_id collides across regions. Supplement G — SOAR: pass cluster_id, vendor, job_id, severity to playbooks without embedding vault secrets. Supplement H — Observability pillar: keep dashboards under the same app prefix for access control inheritance. Supplement I — Regression tests: after technical add-on upgrades, run fieldsummary weekly for each sourcetype list. Supplement J — Poll jitter: stagger pollers to avoid beating the same API second. Supplement K — IPv6: if Prism or HX Connect serves IPv6-only, confirm forwarder DNS and firewall paths. Supplement L — Retention: disk_balance streams can be dense; consider seven-day hot, thirty-day warm for cost. Supplement M — Audit: pair saved search schedule logs with alert_triggered for chain of custody. Supplement N — Escalation: critical stall plus skew should not wait for business hours if freeze is inactive. Supplement O — Training drills: rehearse telling proactive rebalance apart from resync language used in war rooms. Supplement P — Patch windows: expect temporary skew spikes during rolling reboots; set freezes. Supplement Q — vSAN threshold defaults near thirty percent are guides; governance may tune per cluster tier. Supplement R — Azure jobs: Optimize-Volume may surface under a different JobType string; align filters. Supplement S — Collector heartbeats: add hci:poller:heartbeat events keyed by cluster_id to detect gaps independent of storage vendors. Supplement T — Field quotas: if extract exceeds index field limits, use selective INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS and KV mode. Supplement U — Naming drift: after firmware refresh, diff default transforms before merge. Supplement V — Governance cadence: review hci_rebalance_owner quarterly with capacity management. Supplement W — Evidence packs: store alert CSV with timestamp and cluster_id for internal auditors. Supplement X — Stretched clusters: site-pair context may require forked SPL; document separately. Supplement Y — Licensing: curator poll density affects ingest cost; justify cadence in the capacity workbook. Supplement Z — Closure: relate to UC-19.2.8 for initiating skew conversations without confusing them with failure timelines in UC-19.2.9 and UC-19.2.30.

Appendix AA — Long-form staging plan for regulated customers: stage one vendor per week, keep alerting in report-only mode until hci_rebalance_owner rows exist for every production cluster_id, then enable paging on critical only for a pilot site, then expand. Archive each stage gate email with the search head captain.

Appendix AB — Metric store note: if you route nutanix:curator into a metrics index, fork a small curated summary index for this multisearch until raw fields are guaranteed available; premature metricization breaks coalesce chains when keys become metric_name instead of JSON paths.

Appendix AC — vSAN language control: call the VMware feed proactive rebalance everywhere in tickets so operators do not chase UC-19.2.30 resync runbooks by mistake; keep deep links to the proactive health view in your portal template.

Appendix AD — HX Connect throttling: when API returns HTTP 429, back off exponentially and post a hci:poller:heartbeat warning row so absence-of-data does not look like a healthy idle cluster.

Appendix AE — Windows remoting hardening: constrained endpoints for Get-StorageJob should still expose percent complete and job identifiers; document the exact capability list in the server build standard.

Appendix AF — Dual-stack networking: when Prism answers on IPv4 but HEC needs IPv6, pin DNS separately for poller hosts versus Splunk indexers.

Appendix AG — Upgrade windows: after vCenter upgrades, revalidate that ProactiveRebalanceRunning still surfaces in PowerCLI; VMware occasionally shifts property bags between maintenance packs.

Appendix AH — Tabletop script: once per quarter, walk one warning row from alert to vendor screenshot in under twenty minutes; if not, fix lookup ownership and drilldown links.

Appendix AI — Cost guardrail: rebalance job telemetry is cheap compared to full perf streams; still cap pollers at the slowest frequency that satisfies stall math to avoid unnecessary license churn.

Appendix AJ — Executive brevity: export a monthly one-page PDF listing clusters where rebalance_jobs_completed_7d fell below internal minimums even if severity never exceeded info.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.15 HCI proactive scheduled rebalance progress and per-disk skew on healthy clusters; distinct from UC-19.2.30 resync backlog, UC-19.2.9 failure rebuild, UC-19.2.8 steady skew without job context. Tunables: hci_rebalance_owner.csv (target_max_eta_hours default 12, target_max_disk_skew_pct default 15, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct default 30, target_max_stall_hours default 4). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes to info when active. Each arm: earliest=-25h@h latest=@h. Align streamstats window=24 with poller cadence.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:scan" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:job") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, scan_id, scanId, curator_job_id, curatorJobId, task_id, taskId, "nutanix_curator"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, rebalancePercent, percent_complete, scan_percent_complete, progress_pct, pct_complete, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(job_started_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(started_ts),10), relative_time(now(), "-25h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster_config") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, proactive_job_id, "vsan_proactive"))
      | eval pr_run=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceRunning, proactive_rebalance_running, proactiveRunning, "0")), 10)
      | eval pr_iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceMaxIOPS, proactive_rebalance_max_iops, max_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval cfg_thr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ProactiveRebalanceThresholdPct, proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct, proactive_threshold_pct, vsan_proactive_threshold_pct, "")), 10)
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, proactive_pct_complete, pct_complete, if((pr_run>0), 50, 0))), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(job_started_epoch),10), relative_time(now(), "-2h"))
      | eval proactive_cfg_thr=if(isnotnull(cfg_thr) AND cfg_thr>0, cfg_thr, null())
      | eval proactive_mark=if(pr_run>0, 1, 0)
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts latest(pr_run) AS pr_run latest(pr_iops) AS pr_iops latest(proactive_cfg_thr) AS proactive_cfg_thr max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance" earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, rebalance_job_id, operation_id, "hyperflex_rebalance"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, rebalancePercent, progress_pct, percentComplete, completion_pct, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), tonumber(tostring(started_ts),10), relative_time(now(), "-6h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, clusterId, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval job_id=toString(coalesce(job_id, jobId, job_guid, JobId, Name, storage_job_id, "s2d_optimize"))
      | eval rebalance_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(rebalance_pct, PercentComplete, percent_complete, progress_pct, "0")), 10)
      | eval job_started_ts=coalesce(tonumber(tostring(start_epoch),10), relative_time(now(), "-12h"))
      | eval proactive_mark=1
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(rebalance_pct) AS rebalance_pct min(job_started_ts) AS job_started_ts max(proactive_mark) AS proactive_mark latest(vendor) AS vendor BY cluster_id job_id vendor ]
| where (len(cluster_id)>0) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| search (index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator:disk_balance" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterUuid, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
     | eval vendor=case(sourcetype="nutanix:curator:disk_balance", "nutanix", sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:phys_disk", "azure_stack_hci", "unknown")
     | eval disk_used_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(disk_used_pct, used_pct, utilization_pct, percent_used, SizeUsedPercent, "0")), 10)
     | where disk_used_pct>0
     | stats range(disk_used_pct) AS rebalance_disk_skew_pct BY cluster_id vendor ]
| fillnull value=0 rebalance_disk_skew_pct
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| search (index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=vmware OR index=cisco OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="nutanix:curator" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:scan" OR sourcetype="nutanix:curator:job" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance" OR sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:optimize_job" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:s2d:storage_pool_balance") earliest=-7d@d latest=@h
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterId, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
     | eval vendor=case(match(sourcetype, "nutanix"), "nutanix", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:proactive_rebalance", "vsan", sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:rebalance", "hyperflex", match(sourcetype, "azure_stack_hci"), "azure_stack_hci", "unknown")
     | eval completed=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(completion_flag, job_complete, is_complete, scan_completed, Completed, status_code, "0")), 10)
     | where (completed>0) OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(status, job_state, JobState, ""))), "success|complete|succeeded")
     | stats count AS rebalance_jobs_completed_7d BY cluster_id vendor ]
| fillnull value=0 rebalance_jobs_completed_7d
| eval job_started_ts=if(isnull(job_started_ts), relative_time(now(), "-24h"), job_started_ts)
| eval rebalance_job_age_hours=round((now()-job_started_ts)/3600.0, 2)
| eval proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(proactive_cfg_thr, "")), 10)
| eval proactive_disabled=if((vendor="vsan") AND (pr_run=0), 1, 0)
| eventstats avg(rebalance_pct) AS cluster_mean_rebalance_pct max(rebalance_disk_skew_pct) AS cluster_context_max_skew BY cluster_id
| sort 0 cluster_id job_id vendor _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t first(rebalance_pct) AS p_first last(rebalance_pct) AS p_last first(_time) AS t_first last(_time) AS t_last count AS win_cnt BY cluster_id job_id vendor
| eval slope=if((t_last>t_first) AND ((t_last-t_first)>60), (p_last-p_first)/((t_last-t_first)/60.0), null())
| eval rebalance_eta_minutes=if((isnotnull(slope)) AND (slope>0.02) AND (p_last<100), round(((100-p_last)/slope), 2), null())
| streamstats window=24 current=t min(rebalance_pct) AS rmin max(rebalance_pct) AS rmax BY cluster_id job_id vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_rebalance_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_eta_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_eta_hours, "12")), 10)
     | eval target_max_disk_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_disk_skew_pct, "15")), 10)
     | eval target_min_proactive_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_proactive_threshold_pct, "30")), 10)
     | eval target_max_stall_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stall_hours, "4")), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_eta_hours target_max_disk_skew_pct target_min_proactive_threshold_pct target_max_stall_hours change_freeze_until ]
| eval tmaxm=target_max_eta_hours*60.0
| eval stall_need=if(target_max_stall_hours>24, 24, target_max_stall_hours)
| eval rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator=if((win_cnt>=stall_need) AND (isnotnull(rmin)) AND (rmax=rmin) AND (rmax>0) AND (rmax<95), 1, 0)
| eval proactive_thr=if(isnotnull(proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct) AND proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct>0, proactive_rebalance_threshold_pct, target_min_proactive_threshold_pct)
| eval proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator=if((vendor="vsan") AND (proactive_disabled=1) AND (rebalance_disk_skew_pct>proactive_thr), 1, 0)
| eval sev_base=case(((rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator=1) AND (rebalance_disk_skew_pct>=target_max_disk_skew_pct)), "critical", ((isnotnull(rebalance_eta_minutes)) AND (rebalance_eta_minutes>tmaxm)) OR (proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator=1), "high", ((rebalance_disk_skew_pct>=(target_max_disk_skew_pct-5)) AND (rebalance_pct<95)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev_base)
| table vendor cluster_id job_id rebalance_pct rebalance_eta_minutes rebalance_disk_skew_pct rebalance_jobs_stalled_indicator proactive_disabled_with_skew_indicator severity owner_team change_freeze_until
```

## Visualization

Trellis single value rebalance_pct by cluster_id; twenty-four hour sparkline of rebalance_pct per job_id; overlay rebalance_disk_skew_pct against lookup limits; auxiliary single value rebalance_jobs_completed_7d; alert table matching the closing projection with drilldowns to vendor consoles.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Nutanix (Splunkbase 5076)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [Microsoft Learn — Run Storage Maintenance Tasks (Optimize-StoragePool, Optimize-Volume)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/storage/storage-spaces/storage-maintenance)
